component.name: Breadcrumbs
component.description: A navigation trail that shows the user's location in a hierarchy and lets them jump back to ancestor pages. Root → current, separated by '/'.
component.note: nav > ol > li with item links; the last item is the current page (aria-current, non-link). Items neutral.600 → neutral.800 + underline on hover. Inter 14/20, 8px gaps. Synced to Figma '❖ Breadcrumbs'.

useCases: Show location within a multi-level hierarchy (Home / Section / Page)
Provide quick navigation back to ancestor pages
Sit at the top of a detail page or inside a Page Header

commonPatterns.name: Standard trail
commonPatterns.description: Ancestors are links; the current page is the last item.

commonPatterns.name: Truncated labels
commonPatterns.description: Cap long labels with an ellipsis so the trail stays on one line.

commonPatterns.name: Custom separator
commonPatterns.description: Swap the '/' for a chevron or other glyph.

antiPatterns.scenario: Making the current (last) page a link
antiPatterns.reason: Linking to the current page is redundant and confuses orientation.
antiPatterns.alternative: Leave the last item without href; it renders as the current page (aria-current).

antiPatterns.reason: Breadcrumbs are a secondary wayfinding aid, not the main nav.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the app's primary nav (Menu / Page Layout side nav).

antiPatterns.scenario: Deep trails with many long labels on one line
antiPatterns.reason: Overflows and is hard to scan.
antiPatterns.alternative: Truncate with maxItemWidth, or collapse middle items.

antiPatterns.reason: A trail of one conveys no hierarchy.

slots.items: Required — ordered array of { label, href? }; last item is the current page

interactions.hover: Link crumbs darken to neutral.800 and underline
interactions.focus: 2px forest.700 outline on the focused link
interactions.current: Last item is non-interactive and marked aria-current='page'
interactions.truncation: maxItemWidth caps each label with an ellipsis

accessibility.role: nav landmark with aria-label (default 'Breadcrumb'); ordered list of items
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab through the ancestor links; Enter activates
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a navigation landmark; separators are aria-hidden

figmaDivergences.implementation: colors.text.neutral.default (neutral.600, #525252) — the value defined on the BreadcrumbItem part
figmaDivergences.reason: #6b6e76 is an Atlassian gray not in the Athena palette; the item part itself specifies neutral.600.

aiHints.context: Reach for Breadcrumbs to show where a page sits in a hierarchy and let users step back up. Put ancestors as links and the current page last (no href). Place at the top of detail pages or in a Page Header. Not a replacement for primary navigation.
neverUseWhen: There is no hierarchy / only one level — omit them
It would act as the primary navigation — use the app nav
The current page would be a link — leave the last item without href
